name: GitHub Push → CI Status → Slack Notification
description: Receives GitHub push webhook events, validates the HMAC signature, extracts commit metadata, and posts a formatted notification to Slack. Shows how to wire an incoming webhook to a processing workflow.

config.SYSTEM_PROMPT: You receive a GitHub commit object. Return a single concise Slack-formatted line: "*[branch]* <commit message> by <author> (<short_sha>)". No prose, no markdown fences.

webhook_trigger.description: This workflow is triggered by an incoming GitHub webhook — NOT by a cron schedule. After publishing, call create_webhook with workflow_id to get the inbound URL, then paste it into GitHub → Settings → Webhooks with Content-Type: application/json.
setup_steps: Call create_webhook with {workflow_id, secret_key_path: 'github/webhook_secret'} to get the inbound URL
In your GitHub repo: Settings → Webhooks → Add webhook
Set Payload URL to the inbound URL, Content type: application/json, Secret: the value at github/webhook_secret
Select 'Just the push event' (or your desired events)
Save — GitHub will immediately deliver a ping event to verify connectivity

required_secrets.description: Random string used to sign GitHub webhook payloads (generate with: openssl rand -hex 20)
Slack bot token with chat:write scope
Anthropic API key for commit message formatting

replacements.config_delta: Use for Gmail-triggered workflows instead. For GitHub events, keep Slack Webhook Listener and configure SIGNING_SECRET_PATH to 'github/webhook_secret'.
Replace CHANNEL+BOT_TOKEN with WEBHOOK_URL_SECRET='teams/webhook_url'. Use when your engineering team uses Teams instead of Slack.
Replace Slack notification with a PagerDuty incident trigger. Set INTEGRATION_KEY_SECRET, SUMMARY (e.g. 'Push to main by {{author}}'), ACTION='trigger'. Use when push events should page on-call.
